Title: Synthetic biology stretching the realms of possibility in wine yeast research

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, initiating entry into the phenylpropanoid pathway toward raspberry ketone production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor and aroma compound conferring raspberry-like character to wine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables de novo raspberry ketone biosynthesis in semisynthetic wine yeast when combined with the engineered pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway engineering; gene integration into HO locus; wine fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard wine fermentation conditions in Chardonnay grape juice
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with other flavor pathways to create customized wine yeast strains with targeted red-fruit aroma profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article summarizing Lee et al. (2016); gene was codon-optimized and integrated into the yeast HO locus.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtPAL was one of the codon-optimized heterologous genes installed in wine yeast AWRI1631. 2. It supplied the initial phenylpropanoid entry step from phenylalanine toward p-coumaric acid. 3. In combination with the other introduced enzymes, it contributed to raspberry ketone accumulation above the predicted sensory threshold in Chardonnay fermentation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid at the 4-position to form p-coumaric acid.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor and aroma compound conferring raspberry-like character to wine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides p-coumaric acid formation capacity in engineered wine yeast for downstream raspberry ketone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway engineering; gene integration into HO locus; wine fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard wine fermentation conditions in Chardonnay grape juice
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of synthetic flavor-biosynthesis modules for custom yeast strains in wine fermentation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article; described as one of the codon-optimized genes integrated at the HO locus in AWRI1631.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtC4H converted cinnamate to p-coumaric acid in the engineered pathway. 2. This step linked upstream aromatic amino acid metabolism to raspberry ketone biosynthesis. 3. Together with the other introduced enzymes, it supported production of raspberry ketone at sensory-relevant levels during Chardonnay fermentation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates p-coumaric acid to coumaroyl-CoA for condensation with malonyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor and aroma compound conferring raspberry-like character to wine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies activated phenylpropanoid intermediate required for benzalacetone formation and downstream raspberry ketone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway engineering; fusion construct design; gene integration into HO locus; wine fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard wine fermentation conditions in Chardonnay grape juice
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key component for reconstructing phenylpropanoid-derived aroma pathways in industrial yeast strains.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review specifies that Pc4CL2 was fused via a rigid linker to RpBAS.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pc4CL2 generated coumaroyl-CoA from p-coumaric acid in the engineered yeast. 2. The enzyme was deployed as a fusion with RpBAS to channel intermediates. 3. This design contributed to efficient raspberry ketone production in Chardonnay must fermentation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form benzalacetone, a direct precursor in raspberry ketone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor and aroma compound conferring raspberry-like character to wine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the committed condensation step toward raspberry ketone biosynthesis in semisynthetic wine yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway engineering; fusion construct design; gene integration into HO locus; wine fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard wine fermentation conditions in Chardonnay grape juice
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for designing red-fruit aroma traits in industrial wine yeast and for pathway stacking with precursor-supply modules.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review describes RpBAS as fused to Pc4CL2 with a rigid linker; final reduction to raspberry ketone relied on host native activities complementing engineered steps.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RpBAS catalyzed the condensation step that generated benzalacetone from coumaroyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA. 2. It was introduced as part of a Pc4CL2-RpBAS fusion in AWRI1631. 3. Together with native host activities, this step enabled de novo raspberry ketone formation above sensory threshold in Chardonnay fermentations.
